channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Diamagnetic Meissner response of odd-frequency superconducting pairing from quantum geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Ankita Bhattacharya and Annica M. Black-Schaffer

Odd-frequency superconductivity is ubiquitous in multiband systems but is commonly believed to yield a paramagnetic Meissner response and thus be unstable. The authors show here that this paradigm breaks down once quantum geometry is considered. For general two-band superconductors, the authors establish that the geometric interband contribution to the Meissner response from odd-frequency pairing is in fact diamagnetic, thus stabilizing odd-frequency superconductivity. In flat-band systems this geometric contribution can even dominate the Meissner response, which identifies flat-band materials as promising platforms for robust odd-frequency superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ankita Bhattacharya and Annica M. Black-Schaffer

Odd-frequency superconductivity is ubiquitous in multiband systems but is commonly believed to yield a paramagnetic Meissner response and thus be unstable. The authors show here that this paradigm breaks down once quantum geometry is considered. For general two-band superconductors, the authors establish that the geometric interband contribution to the Meissner response from odd-frequency pairing is in fact diamagnetic, thus stabilizing odd-frequency superconductivity. In flat-band systems this geometric contribution can even dominate the Meissner response, which identifies flat-band materials as promising platforms for robust odd-frequency superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Diamagnetic Meissner response of odd-frequency superconducting pairing from quantum geometry
item.dc:creator: Ankita Bhattacharya and Annica M. Black-Schaffer

item.title: Nematic enhancement of superconductivity in multilayer graphene via quantum geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Gal Shavit

Multilayer graphene materials have recently emerged as a fascinating versatile platform for correlated electron phenomena, hosting superconductivity, fractional quantum Hall states, and correlated insulating phases. A particularly striking experimental observation is the recurring correlation betwee…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gal Shavit

Multilayer graphene materials have recently emerged as a fascinating versatile platform for correlated electron phenomena, hosting superconductivity, fractional quantum Hall states, and correlated insulating phases. A particularly striking experimental observation is the recurring correlation betwee…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026

item.title: Majorana flat bands in the vortex line of superconducting Weyl semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): Zhicheng Zhang and Kou-Han Ma

We report the emergence of Majorana flat bands (MFBs) in the vortex line of superconducting (SC) time-reversal symmetry-breaking Weyl semimetals. By considering a Weyl semimetal as a stack of Chern insulators with varying Chern numbers along one ($z$) direction, we decompose the vortex bound states …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhicheng Zhang and Kou-Han Ma

We report the emergence of Majorana flat bands (MFBs) in the vortex line of superconducting (SC) time-reversal symmetry-breaking Weyl semimetals. By considering a Weyl semimetal as a stack of Chern insulators with varying Chern numbers along one (z) direction, we decompose the vortex bound states of…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Majorana flat bands in the vortex line of superconducting Weyl semimetals
item.dc:creator: Zhicheng Zhang and Kou-Han Ma

item.title: Berry curvature of low-energy excitons in rhombohedral graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Henry Davenport, Frank Schindler, and Johannes Knolle

We investigate low-energy excitons in rhombohedral pentalayer graphene encapsulated by hexagonal boron nitride (hBN/R5G/hBN), focusing on the regime at the experimental twist angle $θ=0.{77}^{∘}$ and with an applied electric field. We introduce a new low-energy two-band model of rhombohedral graphen…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115102] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Henry Davenport, Frank Schindler, and Johannes Knolle

We investigate low-energy excitons in rhombohedral pentalayer graphene encapsulated by hexagonal boron nitride (hBN/R5G/hBN), focusing on the regime at the experimental twist angle θ=0.77∘ and with an applied electric field. We introduce a new low-energy two-band model of rhombohedral graphene that …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115102] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Berry curvature of low-energy excitons in rhombohedral graphene
item.dc:creator: Henry Davenport, Frank Schindler, and Johannes Knolle

item.title: Unified theory of the photovoltaic Hall effect by field- and light-induced Berry curvatures
item.description:
Author(s): Yuta Murotani, Tomohiro Fujimoto, and Ryusuke Matsunaga

Photovoltaic Hall effect, i.e., generation of a photocurrent perpendicular to the bias electric field, is an interesting platform of Berry curvature engineering by external fields. Floquet engineering aims at generation of light-induced Berry curvature associated with topological phase transition in…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115202] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuta Murotani, Tomohiro Fujimoto, and Ryusuke Matsunaga

Photovoltaic Hall effect, i.e., generation of a photocurrent perpendicular to the bias electric field, is an interesting platform of Berry curvature engineering by external fields. Floquet engineering aims at generation of light-induced Berry curvature associated with topological phase transition in…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115202] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Unified theory of the photovoltaic Hall effect by field- and light-induced Berry curvatures

item.title: Electric field induced spin and/or valley polarization in Weiss oscillations of monolayer $1{T}^{′}−{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yang Li, Wen Zeng, and Rui Shen

Monolayer 1$T{}^{′}−{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ exhibits spin- and valley-dependent massive tilted Dirac cones with two velocity correction terms in low-energy effective Hamiltonian. We theoretically investigate the longitudinal diffusive magneto-conductivity of monolayer 1$T{}^{′}−{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ by us…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115301] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yang Li, Wen Zeng, and Rui Shen

Monolayer 1T′−MoS2 exhibits spin- and valley-dependent massive tilted Dirac cones with two velocity correction terms in low-energy effective Hamiltonian. We theoretically investigate the longitudinal diffusive magneto-conductivity of monolayer 1T′−MoS2 by using the linear response theory. It is show…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115301] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Electric field induced spin and/or valley polarization in Weiss oscillations of monolayer $1{T}^{′}−{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Yang Li, Wen Zeng, and Rui Shen

item.title: Quantifying humidity effects on commensurability-dependent friction in bilayer graphene: A generalized registry index model
item.description:
Author(s): Binyang Shao, Xiaoyan Ren, Kun Liu, Lili Zhang, Yigang Cao, Xingju Zhao, and Shunfang Li

Deciphering the atomistic mechanism governing humidity-dependent interfacial friction remains a fundamental challenge across physics, chemistry, medicine, and materials science. Here, we employ first-principles calculations to systematically investigate the frictional behaviors of bilayer graphene (…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115402] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Binyang Shao, Xiaoyan Ren, Kun Liu, Lili Zhang, Yigang Cao, Xingju Zhao, and Shunfang Li

Deciphering the atomistic mechanism governing humidity-dependent interfacial friction remains a fundamental challenge across physics, chemistry, medicine, and materials science. Here, we employ first-principles calculations to systematically investigate the frictional behaviors of bilayer graphene (…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115402] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantifying humidity effects on commensurability-dependent friction in bilayer graphene: A generalized registry index model

item.title: Boundary topological orders of ($4+1$)d fermionic ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2N}^{F}$ symmetry protected topological states
item.description:
Author(s): Meng Cheng, Juven Wang, and Xinping Yang

We investigate ($3+1$)d topological orders (TOs) in fermionic systems with anomalous ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2N}^{F}$ symmetry, where its ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}^{F}$ subgroup is the fermion parity. Such an anomalous symmetry arises as a discrete subgroup of the chiral U(1) symmetry of $ν$ copies of Weyl fermions …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125105] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Meng Cheng, Juven Wang, and Xinping Yang

We investigate (3+1)d topological orders (TOs) in fermionic systems with anomalous Z2NF symmetry, where its Z2F subgroup is the fermion parity. Such an anomalous symmetry arises as a discrete subgroup of the chiral U(1) symmetry of ν copies of Weyl fermions of the same chirality. Inspired by the cry…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125105] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Boundary topological orders of ($4+1$)d fermionic ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2N}^{F}$ symmetry protected topological states
item.dc:creator: Meng Cheng, Juven Wang, and Xinping Yang

item.title: Synergistic effects of twist angle and substrate coupling on thermal transport in twisted bilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Yiyi Li, Weitao Wang, Weikuan Li, Jianlian Huang, Yanping Qiu, Zhirong Shi, Wei Zhang, Yajuan Cheng, and Shiyun Xiong

The Moiré superlattices in twisted two-dimensional materials give rise to extraordinary electronic and thermal properties. While twist-angle tuning has been extensively explored for electronic applications, its synergistic effects with substrate coupling on thermal transport remain less understood. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125404] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:

The Moiré superlattices in twisted two-dimensional materials give rise to extraordinary electronic and thermal properties. While twist-angle tuning has been extensively explored for electronic applications, its synergistic effects with substrate coupling on thermal transport remain less understood. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125404] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026

item.title: Symmetry-driven transitions between flat bands and Dirac cones in bilayer kagome lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Taylan Gorkan, Dogukan Hazar Ozbey, Cem Sevik, Milorad V. Milošević, and Engin Durgun

Flat bands (FBs) and Dirac cones represent two distinctive features of topological electronic systems; however, a unified mechanism enabling transitions between them has remained elusive to date. Here, we demonstrate a symmetry-governed and tunable transition from flat bands to Dirac cones in AB-sta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125405] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Taylan Gorkan, Dogukan Hazar Ozbey, Cem Sevik, Milorad V. Milošević, and Engin Durgun

Flat bands (FBs) and Dirac cones represent two distinctive features of topological electronic systems; however, a unified mechanism enabling transitions between them has remained elusive to date. Here, we demonstrate a symmetry-governed and tunable transition from flat bands to Dirac cones in AB-sta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125405] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry-driven transitions between flat bands and Dirac cones in bilayer kagome lattices

item.title: Unified topological bulk-entanglement correspondence for the polarization in non-Hermitian systems
item.description:
Author(s): Xudong Zhang, Zhaoyu Sun, and Bin Guo

The non-Hermitian skin effect (NHSE) fundamentally invalidates the conventional bulk-boundary correspondence (BBC), leading topological diagnostics into a crisis. While the non-Bloch polarization ${P}_{β}$, defined on the generalized Brillouin zone, successfully captures momentum-space topology, a p…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100401] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xudong Zhang, Zhaoyu Sun, and Bin Guo

The non-Hermitian skin effect (NHSE) fundamentally invalidates the conventional bulk-boundary correspondence (BBC), leading topological diagnostics into a crisis. While the non-Bloch polarization Pβ, defined on the generalized Brillouin zone, successfully captures momentum-space topology, a purely r…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100401] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Unified topological bulk-entanglement correspondence for the polarization in non-Hermitian systems

item.title: Resonating valence bond pairing energy in graphene by quantum Monte Carlo
item.description:
Author(s): S. Azadi, A. Principi, T. D. Kühne, and M. S. Bahramy

Whether short-range electron pairing can be stabilized in graphene remains a central question because pristine graphene is a semimetal with vanishing density of states at the Dirac point. We show that geometry alone can enhance short-range pairing correlations in confined graphene. Using real-space …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121101] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. Azadi, A. Principi, T. D. Kühne, and M. S. Bahramy

Whether short-range electron pairing can be stabilized in graphene remains a central question because pristine graphene is a semimetal with vanishing density of states at the Dirac point. We show that geometry alone can enhance short-range pairing correlations in confined graphene. Using real-space …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121101] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Resonating valence bond pairing energy in graphene by quantum Monte Carlo

item.title: Topological phases in non-Hermitian nonlinear-eigenvalue systems
item.description:
Author(s): Yu-Peng Ma, Ming-Jian Gao, and Jun-Hong An

The discovery of topological phases has ushered in a new era of condensed matter physics and revealed a variety of natural and artificial materials. They obey the bulk-boundary correspondence (BBC), which guarantees the emergence of boundary states with nonzero topological invariants in the bulk. Wi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121401] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu-Peng Ma, Ming-Jian Gao, and Jun-Hong An

The discovery of topological phases has ushered in a new era of condensed matter physics and revealed a variety of natural and artificial materials. They obey the bulk-boundary correspondence (BBC), which guarantees the emergence of boundary states with nonzero topological invariants in the bulk. Wi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121401] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological phases in non-Hermitian nonlinear-eigenvalue systems

item.title: Statistical study of atomic, electronic, and mechanical properties of amorphous graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Jun Chai, Mingping He, Hangjia Shen, Pan Liu, Jia Zhang, and Yi-Yang Sun

Amorphous graphene (a-G), the disordered counterpart of crystalline graphene (c-G), holds promise for flexible electronics, catalysis, and energy storage, yet its atomic-scale structure and property origins remain elusive. Here, we introduce a helium potential-wall method within ab initio molecular …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054203] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jun Chai, Mingping He, Hangjia Shen, Pan Liu, Jia Zhang, and Yi-Yang Sun

Amorphous graphene (a-G), the disordered counterpart of crystalline graphene (c-G), holds promise for flexible electronics, catalysis, and energy storage, yet its atomic-scale structure and property origins remain elusive. Here, we introduce a helium potential-wall method within ab initio molecular …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054203] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026

item.title: Relationship between local hydride ion dynamics and ionic conductivity in ${\mathrm{LaH}}_{3−2x}{\mathrm{O}}_{x}$ inferred from $μ\mathrm{SR}$
item.description:
Author(s): M. Hiraishi, S. Takeshita, H. Okabe, K. M. Kojima, A. Koda, S. Iimura, K. Fukui, H. Hosono, and R. Kadono

We performed muon spin rotation and relaxation ($μ\mathrm{SR}$) experiments to investigate the microscopic mechanism behind the high ionic conductivity ($σ$) exhibited by hydride (${\mathrm{H}}^{−}$) ions in lanthanum hydroxide ${\mathrm{LaH}}_{3−2x}{\mathrm{O}}_{x}$. The $μ\mathrm{SR}$ spectra obse…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064312] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Hiraishi, S. Takeshita, H. Okabe, K. M. Kojima, A. Koda, S. Iimura, K. Fukui, H. Hosono, and R. Kadono

We performed muon spin rotation and relaxation (μSR) experiments to investigate the microscopic mechanism behind the high ionic conductivity (σ) exhibited by hydride (H−) ions in lanthanum hydroxide LaH3−2xOx. The μSR spectra observed at 5–300 K in a sample with x≈0.25 consist primarily of two compo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064312] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Relationship between local hydride ion dynamics and ionic conductivity in ${\mathrm{LaH}}_{3−2x}{\mathrm{O}}_{x}$ inferred from $μ\mathrm{SR}$

item.title: Disorder-induced spin-cluster magnetism in a doped kagome spin liquid candidate
item.description:
Author(s): Arnab Seth, Joseph C. Prestigiacomo, Aini Xu, Zhenyuan Zeng, Trevor D. Ford, B. S. Shivaram, Shiliang Li, Patrick A. Lee, and Itamar Kimchi

The search for new quantum spin liquid materials relies on systems with strong frustration such as spins on an ideal kagome lattice. However, lattice imperfections can have substantial effects which are as yet not well understood. In recent work, the two-dimensional kagome system ${\mathrm{YCu}}_{3}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064441] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Arnab Seth, Joseph C. Prestigiacomo, Aini Xu, Zhenyuan Zeng, Trevor D. Ford, B. S. Shivaram, Shiliang Li, Patrick A. Lee, and Itamar Kimchi

The search for new quantum spin liquid materials relies on systems with strong frustration such as spins on an ideal kagome lattice. However, lattice imperfections can have substantial effects which are as yet not well understood. In recent work, the two-dimensional kagome system YCu3(OH)6[(ClxBr(1−…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064441] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Disorder-induced spin-cluster magnetism in a doped kagome spin liquid candidate

item.title: Four-spin interactions as a route to multiple-$Q$ topological magnetic order
item.description:
Author(s): Kazuki Okigami and Satoru Hayami

We investigate the role of four-spin interactions in stabilizing multiple-$Q$ topological spin textures and demonstrate their ability to realize a skyrmion crystal. While such higher-order interactions are known to be important, their intricate nature makes systematic model construction challenging.…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064442] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kazuki Okigami and Satoru Hayami

We investigate the role of four-spin interactions in stabilizing multiple-Q topological spin textures and demonstrate their ability to realize a skyrmion crystal. While such higher-order interactions are known to be important, their intricate nature makes systematic model construction challenging. T…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064442] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026

item.title: Spin-orbit coupled periodic Anderson model: Kondo-Dirac semimetal and orbital-selective antiferromagnetic semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): Sebastião dos Anjos Sousa-Júnior, Julián Faúndez, and Rubem Mondaini

We investigate the periodic Anderson model composed of an itinerant $c$ band and a strongly localized $f$ band, featuring on-site electron-electron interactions in the $f$ orbitals. The two bands interact via a hybridization term with spin-orbit coupling, which enables spin-flip processes. In the no…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085149] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sebastião dos Anjos Sousa-Júnior, Julián Faúndez, and Rubem Mondaini

We investigate the periodic Anderson model composed of an itinerant c band and a strongly localized f band, featuring on-site electron-electron interactions in the f orbitals. The two bands interact via a hybridization term with spin-orbit coupling, which enables spin-flip processes. In the noninter…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085149] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-orbit coupled periodic Anderson model: Kondo-Dirac semimetal and orbital-selective antiferromagnetic semimetal

item.title: Structural origin of resonant diffraction in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Connor A. Occhialini, Christie Nelson, Alessandro Bombardi, Shiyu Fan, Raul Acevedo-Esteves, Riccardo Comin, Dmitri N. Basov, Maki Musashi, Masashi Kawasaki, Masaki Uchida, Hoydoo You, J. F. Mitchell, Valentina Bisogni, Claudio Mazzoli, and Jonathan Pelliciari

We report Ru ${L}_{3}$-edge resonant X-ray diffraction studies on single-crystal and (001)-oriented epitaxial films of ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$. We investigate the distinct $\mathbf{Q}=(100)$ and (001) Bragg-forbidden reflections as a function of incident energy, azimuthal angle, and temperature. The re…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060411] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Connor A. Occhialini, Christie Nelson, Alessandro Bombardi, Shiyu Fan, Raul Acevedo-Esteves, Riccardo Comin, Dmitri N. Basov, Maki Musashi, Masashi Kawasaki, Masaki Uchida, Hoydoo You, J. F. Mitchell, Valentina Bisogni, Claudio Mazzoli, and Jonathan Pelliciari

We report Ru L3-edge resonant X-ray diffraction studies on single-crystal and (001)-oriented epitaxial films of RuO2. We investigate the distinct Q=(100) and (001) Bragg-forbidden reflections as a function of incident energy, azimuthal angle, and temperature. The results show that the observed reson…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060411] Published Thu Feb 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Structural origin of resonant diffraction in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$

item.title: Orbital and magnetic ordering in the weak ferromagnet vanadium trifluoride ${\mathrm{VF}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Reinhard K. Kremer, Florian Kraus, Patrick Woidy, Gwilherm Nénert, Christina Drahten, and Bachir Ouladdiaf

We report on an investigation of the low-temperature structural, thermal, and magnetic properties of the binary trifluoride ${\mathrm{VF}}_{3}$ by temperature-dependent magnetization, heat capacity, electron paramagnetic resonance, and synchrotron powder and neutron powder diffraction measurements. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054442] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

Author(s): Reinhard K. Kremer, Florian Kraus, Patrick Woidy, Gwilherm Nénert, Christina Drahten, and Bachir Ouladdiaf

We report on an investigation of the low-temperature structural, thermal, and magnetic properties of the binary trifluoride VF3 by temperature-dependent magnetization, heat capacity, electron paramagnetic resonance, and synchrotron powder and neutron powder diffraction measurements. At room temperat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054442] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: Pressure-induced enhancement of magnetism in ${\mathrm{Mn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoteng Huang, Wenfeng Wu, Xianlong Wang, Yonggang Li, Liangjian Zou, and Zhi Zeng

${\mathrm{Mn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{5}$, a layered magnetic topological insulator, provides an ideal platform for studying the pressure-tuned interplay between magnetism and topology. Using first-principles density functional theory and Monte Carlo simulations, we examine its structura…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054443] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoteng Huang, Wenfeng Wu, Xianlong Wang, Yonggang Li, Liangjian Zou, and Zhi Zeng

Mn2Bi2Te5, a layered magnetic topological insulator, provides an ideal platform for studying the pressure-tuned interplay between magnetism and topology. Using first-principles density functional theory and Monte Carlo simulations, we examine its structural, magnetic, and electronic evolution under …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054443] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Pressure-induced enhancement of magnetism in ${\mathrm{Mn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Xiaoteng Huang, Wenfeng Wu, Xianlong Wang, Yonggang Li, Liangjian Zou, and Zhi Zeng

item.title: Higher-order topological superconductivity in type-II time-reversal symmetric Weyl semimetals with a hybrid pairing
item.description:
Author(s): Junkang Huang, Z. D. Wang, and Tao Zhou

We employed the self-consistent method on a two-orbital type-II time-reversal-symmetric Weyl semimetal, revealing a hybrid pairing of singlet $s$ wave and triplet $p$ wave. We present a detailed analysis of the normal-state electronic structure and the self-consistent results. Our findings indicate …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054523] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junkang Huang, Z. D. Wang, and Tao Zhou

We employed the self-consistent method on a two-orbital type-II time-reversal-symmetric Weyl semimetal, revealing a hybrid pairing of singlet s wave and triplet p wave. We present a detailed analysis of the normal-state electronic structure and the self-consistent results. Our findings indicate that…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054523] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Higher-order topological superconductivity in type-II time-reversal symmetric Weyl semimetals with a hybrid pairing

item.title: Entanglement manifestation of link topology in a non-Hermitian lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Guoying Zhang, Li Wang, and Shu Chen

Although the homotopy-knot theory has been utilized to implement effective topological classification for non-Hermitian systems, more profound physical implications underlying distinct knot or link topologies are yet to be fully elucidated. In this work, we propose a one-dimensional non-Hermitian fo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064115] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guoying Zhang, Li Wang, and Shu Chen

Although the homotopy-knot theory has been utilized to implement effective topological classification for non-Hermitian systems, more profound physical implications underlying distinct knot or link topologies are yet to be fully elucidated. In this work, we propose a one-dimensional non-Hermitian fo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064115] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: Interplay of localization and topology in disordered dimerized arrays of Rydberg atoms
item.description:
Author(s): Maksym Prodius, Adith Sai Aramthottil, and Jakub Zakrzewski

Rydberg tweezer arrays provide a platform for realizing spin-1/2 Hamiltonians with long-range tunneling that decays as a power law with distance. We numerically investigate the effects of positional disorder and dimerization on the properties of excited states in such a one-dimensional system. Our m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064205] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Maksym Prodius, Adith Sai Aramthottil, and Jakub Zakrzewski

Rydberg tweezer arrays provide a platform for realizing spin-1/2 Hamiltonians with long-range tunneling that decays as a power law with distance. We numerically investigate the effects of positional disorder and dimerization on the properties of excited states in such a one-dimensional system. Our m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064205] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: High bosonic Bott index and transport of multiband topological magnons
item.description:
Author(s): Kai-Tao Huang and X. S. Wang

Magnons are bosonic quasiparticles in magnetically ordered systems. Bosonic Bott index has been affirmed as a real-space topological invariant for a two-band ferromagnetic model. In this work, we theoretically investigate the topology and transport of magnons in a multiband bosonic kagome ferromagne…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064439] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kai-Tao Huang and X. S. Wang

Magnons are bosonic quasiparticles in magnetically ordered systems. Bosonic Bott index has been affirmed as a real-space topological invariant for a two-band ferromagnetic model. In this work, we theoretically investigate the topology and transport of magnons in a multiband bosonic kagome ferromagne…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064439] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: Multilayer stacking ferroelectricity in two-dimensional materials with Bravais lattice symmetry: Theory and applications
item.description:
Author(s): Jiaqi Xin, Yaguang Guo, and Qian Wang

The sliding of van der Waals (vdW) bilayers can induce electric polarization in certain nonpolar two-dimensional (2D) materials. However, this mechanism does not apply to 2D materials with Bravais lattice symmetry (e.g., graphene), where the atomic structure and lattice symmetry have the same point …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075310] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiaqi Xin, Yaguang Guo, and Qian Wang

The sliding of van der Waals (vdW) bilayers can induce electric polarization in certain nonpolar two-dimensional (2D) materials. However, this mechanism does not apply to 2D materials with Bravais lattice symmetry (e.g., graphene), where the atomic structure and lattice symmetry have the same point …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075310] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: Effect of lattice distortion on the band structure and magnetism in the annealed kagome magnet FeGe
item.description:
Author(s): Ao Zhang, Run Yang, Yiyuan Zhu, Zhixiang Shi, Xueliang Wu, Aifeng Wang, Bingke Ji, Yaomin Dai, and Jianzhou Zhao

In this work, combined with the optical spectroscopy and the first-principles calculations, we investigated the effect of lattice distortion on the band structure and magnetism of FeGe annealed at different temperatures. Our observations reveal that the sample annealed at $320{\phantom{\rule{0.16em}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085146] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ao Zhang, Run Yang, Yiyuan Zhu, Zhixiang Shi, Xueliang Wu, Aifeng Wang, Bingke Ji, Yaomin Dai, and Jianzhou Zhao

In this work, combined with the optical spectroscopy and the first-principles calculations, we investigated the effect of lattice distortion on the band structure and magnetism of FeGe annealed at different temperatures. Our observations reveal that the sample annealed at 320∘C experienced dramatic …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085146] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: Fragility of antichiral edge states in a modified Haldane model
item.description:
Author(s): Shuguang Cheng

Antichiral edge states, proposed as protected copropagating modes in the modified Haldane model, have since been studied in diverse systems. Through numerical calculation, we reveal that such antichiral edge states are unstable to disorder: they exhibit a shorter localization length than the bulk st…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085303] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuguang Cheng

Antichiral edge states, proposed as protected copropagating modes in the modified Haldane model, have since been studied in diverse systems. Through numerical calculation, we reveal that such antichiral edge states are unstable to disorder: they exhibit a shorter localization length than the bulk st…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085303] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: Tunable interplay of strain and magnetism in $\mathrm{Co}/{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Qoimatul Mustaghfiroh, Su Jin Kim, Ikhwan Nur Rahman, Anabil Gayen, Fathiya Rahmani, Min Choi, Yeon Suk Choi, Seung-Young Park, Chuyang Liu, Yujing Zhang, Hyun Seok Lee, and Dong-Hyun Kim

We report our direct investigation on a built-in strain and its effect on magnetic properties in $\mathrm{Co}/{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ heterostructure. With variation of Co thickness, tensile strain is built in monolayer ${\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ ranging from 0.7% to 1.9%. Magneto-optical Kerr microscopy reve…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054440] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qoimatul Mustaghfiroh, Su Jin Kim, Ikhwan Nur Rahman, Anabil Gayen, Fathiya Rahmani, Min Choi, Yeon Suk Choi, Seung-Young Park, Chuyang Liu, Yujing Zhang, Hyun Seok Lee, and Dong-Hyun Kim

We report our direct investigation on a built-in strain and its effect on magnetic properties in Co/MoS2 heterostructure. With variation of Co thickness, tensile strain is built in monolayer MoS2 ranging from 0.7% to 1.9%. Magneto-optical Kerr microscopy reveals distinct changes in microscopic magne…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054440] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunable interplay of strain and magnetism in $\mathrm{Co}/{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Qoimatul Mustaghfiroh, Su Jin Kim, Ikhwan Nur Rahman, Anabil Gayen, Fathiya Rahmani, Min Choi, Yeon Suk Choi, Seung-Young Park, Chuyang Liu, Yujing Zhang, Hyun Seok Lee, and Dong-Hyun Kim

item.title: Spin-polarized Dirac points and near-flat bands in ${\mathrm{FePd}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}$ with a one-dimensional ferromagnetic zigzag chain
item.description:
Author(s): Pengfei Ding, Huihui He, Bingxian Shi, Zhixiao He, Xiaoxu Gao, Peng Cheng, Kai Liu, and Shancai Wang

One-dimensional zigzag chains offer a striking platform for hosting Dirac fermions protected by glide reflection symmetry, along with nearly flat electronic bands. Though rich in theoretical promise, these lattices are mostly found as structural fragments rather than in fully formed bulk crystals. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075151] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pengfei Ding, Huihui He, Bingxian Shi, Zhixiao He, Xiaoxu Gao, Peng Cheng, Kai Liu, and Shancai Wang

One-dimensional zigzag chains offer a striking platform for hosting Dirac fermions protected by glide reflection symmetry, along with nearly flat electronic bands. Though rich in theoretical promise, these lattices are mostly found as structural fragments rather than in fully formed bulk crystals. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075151] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-polarized Dirac points and near-flat bands in ${\mathrm{FePd}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}$ with a one-dimensional ferromagnetic zigzag chain

item.title: Magnetotransport across Weyl semimetal grain boundaries
item.description:
Author(s): Haoyang Tian, Vatsal Dwivedi, Adam Yanis Chaou, and Maxim Breitkreiz

A clean interface between two Weyl semimetals features a universal, field-linear tunnel magnetoconductance of $({e}^{2}/h){N}_{\mathrm{ho}}$ per magnetic flux quantum, where ${N}_{\mathrm{ho}}$ is the number of chirality-preserving topological interface Fermi arcs. In this work we show that the line…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075152] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haoyang Tian, Vatsal Dwivedi, Adam Yanis Chaou, and Maxim Breitkreiz

A clean interface between two Weyl semimetals features a universal, field-linear tunnel magnetoconductance of (e2/h)Nho per magnetic flux quantum, where Nho is the number of chirality-preserving topological interface Fermi arcs. In this work we show that the linearity of the magnetoconductance is ro…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075152] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetotransport across Weyl semimetal grain boundaries
item.dc:creator: Haoyang Tian, Vatsal Dwivedi, Adam Yanis Chaou, and Maxim Breitkreiz
